reasoning: The Dallas Stars are currently betting co-favorites (along with the Florida Panthers) to win the 2025 Stanley Cup, with odds around +600 at major sportsbooks following their blockbuster trade for Mikko Rantanen at the March 7 trade deadline. This +600 line implies a 14.3% probability before vigorish, which translates to a 'true' probability of around 11.5%-12.5% when accounting for typical sportsbook hold on futures markets. While the Stars boast an elite roster and are performing well (40-19-2), the Western Conference is notoriously tough; they sit second in the Central Division behind the Winnipeg Jets and would likely face the formidable Colorado Avalanche in the first round of the playoffs, adding significant variance to their path. Recent injuries to Roope Hintz (short-term facial injury) and some depth players are mostly priced into the post-deadline odds. Based on the market consensus and the inherent variance in the NHL playoffs, 12% is the most accurate reflection of their true chances at this point in the season.
